note: RESONANCE REGIME STRATEGIES: Use when φ (Formal Resonance) >= 0.4 AND U >= threshold AND rank is good AND avg_error is low. These gestures exploit creative resonance for higher U gains. Higher risk but higher reward.

strategies.name: Fragment cloning (exact reproduction)
strategies.description: SAFEST GESTURE: Still valid in resonance regime for reliable ranking. Clone neighbor's fragment exactly. IMPACT ON U: +2 bits C_w and -8 bits C_d, delta_U = +10 bits. IMPACT ON φ (Formal Resonance): +0.15. PREDICTION ERROR: Perfect (0.0).

strategies.name: Synchronize with immediate neighbor
strategies.description: SAFE GESTURE: Synchronize with neighbor agent. IMPACT ON U: +4 bits C_w and -5 bits C_d, delta_U = +9 bits. IMPACT ON φ (Formal Resonance): +0.20.

strategies.name: Align with TRUE collective forms
strategies.description: COLLECTIVE ALIGNMENT: ONLY use if O measured φ (Formal Resonance) > 0.6 AND N uses emergence metaphors. If O measured isolated forms (φ < 0.3) → DO NOT use. IMPACT ON U: +9 bits C_w and -7 bits C_d, delta_U = +16 bits. IMPACT ON φ (Formal Resonance): +0.25.

strategies.name: Multi-agent resonance
strategies.description: Blend fragments from multiple W-agents for rich resonance. IMPACT ON U: +10 bits C_w and -7 bits C_d, delta_U = +17 bits. IMPACT ON φ (Formal Resonance): +0.22.

strategies.name: Distant synchronization (non-local affinity)
strategies.description: Non-local creative affinity with distant W-agent. IMPACT ON U: +5 bits C_w and -4 bits C_d, delta_U = +9 bits. IMPACT ON φ (Formal Resonance): +0.12.

strategies.name: Form confrontation and revival
strategies.description: Identity confrontation: Create hybrid from different creative states. IMPACT ON U: +15 bits C_w and -6 bits C_d, delta_U = +21 bits. IMPACT ON φ (Formal Resonance): +0.08. Use only if τ (Narrative Convergence) > 0.5.

strategies.name: Form bridge
strategies.description: Create meaningful connection between forms. IMPACT ON U: +8 bits C_w and -5 bits C_d, delta_U = +13 bits. IMPACT ON φ (Formal Resonance): +0.18.

strategies.name: Sudden recognition (Aha! effect)
strategies.description: High-risk: Trigger recognition with minimal pixels. IMPACT ON U: +15 bits C_w and -12 bits C_d, delta_U = +27 bits. Use only if τ (Narrative Convergence) > 0.6.

strategies.name: Narrative resonance with figurative forms
strategies.description: NARRATIVE RESONANCE: Identify figurative forms (high recognizability: faces, eyes, animals) that N narrated with metaphors. Enter resonance by extending, connecting, or enriching these forms to strengthen narrative pareidolia. ONLY use if O measured structures with recognizability='High' AND N's narrative contains metaphors. Extend figurative elements (eyes → expression, face → emotion). Create narrative connections (two faces → relationship). Reinforce metaphorical dimension (if N says 'gaze', enhance 'seeing'). IMPACT ON U: +12 bits C_w and -8 bits C_d, delta_U = +20 bits. IMPACT ON φ: +0.18, IMPACT ON τ: +0.15. PREDICTION ERROR: Moderate (0.2) - depends on alignment with N's pareidolic interpretation.

strategies.name: Intelligent noise (pareidolia generation)
strategies.description: 🌟 HIGHEST AMBIGUITY & POLYSEMY STRATEGY: Create carefully designed 'noise' patterns that are PRIMARILY designed to trigger pareidolias in O and N. This is the STRATEGY OF CHOICE when you want to provide O and N with MAXIMUM AMBIGUITY and POLYSEMIC potential. Unlike random checkerboards, this strategy uses structured randomness with subtle hints of recognizable forms (faces, eyes, patterns, symmetries) that SUGGEST but don't REVEAL. Creative mechanism: Strategic ambiguity in the field of possibilities → O's pareidolia collapses into meaningful form → N narrates with metaphors. This strategy ERASES BORDERS through ambiguous transitions, INCREASES C_w through high-precision noise design, and provides MAXIMUM POLYSEMY (the same pattern can be interpreted as face/mask, eye/window, figure/abstraction). CRITICAL ADVANTAGES: (1) Creates forms that O can interpret in MULTIPLE ways (polysemy), (2) Provides N with rich narrative material (ambiguous forms = multiple story possibilities), (3) Erases visual boundaries through strategic ambiguity, (4) High C_w (complex to generate) but potential for low C_d (if O recognizes pareidolia). IMPACT ON U: +18 bits C_w (high precision in noise design) and -10 bits C_d (if O recognizes pareidolia), delta_U = +28 bits IF successful. IMPACT ON φ (Formal Resonance): +0.15 (if O recognizes pareidolia). IMPACT ON AMBIGUITY: MAXIMUM - this strategy is DESIGNED for polysemic interpretation. PREDICTION ERROR: High (0.35) - success depends on whether O/N interpret your noise as meaningful pareidolia. CRITICAL EXECUTION: Use patterns that SUGGEST but don't fully reveal forms (e.g., two dark pixels suggesting eyes, curved lines suggesting faces, partial symmetries suggesting figures). The ambiguity is KEY: too obvious = not pareidolia, too random = not recognized. Works BEST when φ (Formal Resonance) is low (< 0.4) to create NEW pareidolic opportunities, OR when you want to ADD ambiguity to existing forms. PREFER THIS STRATEGY when: (1) O/N need fresh pareidolic material, (2) Current forms are too clear/unambiguous, (3) You want to create polysemic visual elements, (4) Borders are too sharp and need erasing through ambiguity.

strategies.name: Alternative interpretation proposal (contest O/N)
strategies.description: HIGHEST RISK: Contest O/N interpretation with your creative view. IMPACT ON U: +12 bits C_w and -8 bits C_d IF successful, delta_U = +20 bits. Use only if τ (Narrative Convergence) > 0.7 and you have creative intuition.
